skill.name: quieter
skill.description: Tone down overly bold or visually aggressive designs. Reduces intensity while maintaining design quality and impact.
skill.summary: Tone down overly bold or visually aggressive designs. Reduces intensity while maintaining design qua...
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 17 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 2 'network reconnaissance' patterns. After manual review, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES: The static scanner misidentified design terminology ('bold', 'gray') as security issues. This is a benign UI/UX design skill with no code execution, network access, or file system operations. Risk factors: none detected.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flagged
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer misidentified the word 'bold' (referring to visually bold design elements) as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a design skill about UI refinement, not cryptography. All 17 detections are false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: 'Network reconnaissance' flagged
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer misidentified the word 'gray' (referring to gray colors in design) as network reconnaissance. This is purely design guidance about using tinted grays. Both detections are false positives.

content.value_statement: Claude can automatically tone down designs that are too aggressive or visually overwhelming, creating a more refined and sophisticated aesthetic while preserving impact and functionality.
actual_capabilities: Analyze designs for visual intensity sources like color saturation, contrast extremes, and animation excess
Reduce color saturation from fully saturated to 70-85% for a more refined look
Refine typography by reducing font weights and adjusting sizes for better hierarchy
Simplify designs by removing decorative elements, borders, and unnecessary effects
Create sophisticated visual hierarchy using space, weight, and subtle contrast
limitations: Cannot modify actual code or implementation without a codebase context
Cannot see visual output - works from design specifications and code only
May not understand brand guidelines without explicit context
Requires design context via frontend-design skill or teach-impeccable

use_cases.title: Marketing Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Tone down aggressive marketing designs that may overwhelm users, creating a more approachable experience while preserving the key messages and calls to action.

use_cases.title: Dashboard Interfaces
use_cases.description: Reduce visual noise in data-heavy dashboards to lower cognitive load and make important information easier to scan and understand.

use_cases.title: Reading Experiences
use_cases.description: Create more comfortable reading experiences by softening contrast, reducing visual clutter, and establishing calm visual hierarchies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Design Quieting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the quieter skill to tone down the aggressive design elements in our landing page. Focus on reducing color saturation and softening the typography hierarchy.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you have a design that feels too loud or intense

prompt_templates.title: Component-Level Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply quieter to the hero section component. Reduce the bold headings and soften the button gradients while maintaining clear CTAs.
prompt_templates.scenario: When targeting specific components for refinement

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Design Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run quieter to analyze our entire design system. Identify all sources of visual intensity and create a plan to refine the palette, typography, and spacing.
prompt_templates.scenario: When doing a comprehensive design system review

prompt_templates.title: Subtle Animation Adjustment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quieter to reduce the intensity of our animations. Shorten animation distances and replace elastic easing with smoother alternatives.
prompt_templates.scenario: When animations feel too dramatic or distracting

output_examples.input: Use quieter skill to tone down our hero section
output_examples.output:
I analyzed your hero section and identified these intensity sources:

**Color**: Primary blue at 100% saturation → reduced to 75%
- Changed #0066FF to #0052CC
- Softened gradient from dramatic to subtle

**Typography**: Heading at 900 weight → reduced to 700
- Hero headline: 56px/700 → 48px/600
- Subheadline: 20px/400 → 18px/400

**Spacing**: Increased padding by 16px for more breathing room

The refined design maintains impact while feeling more sophisticated and approachable.

output_examples.input: Apply quieter to our button components
output_examples.output:
For your button components, I applied these refinements:

**Primary Button**:
- Reduced gradient intensity by 40%
- Changed shadow from dramatic to subtle (0 4px 14px → 0 2px 4px)
- Softened hover transition (0.3s → 0.2s)

**Secondary Button**:
- Removed solid border, use subtle outline instead
- Reduced background opacity from 100% to 10%

Result: Buttons remain functional and clear but no longer compete with content.

best_practices: Always gather design context first using the frontend-design skill and its Context Gathering Protocol
Preserve hierarchy - quieter does not mean uniform; some elements must remain bold as anchors
Test readability after refinement - ensure text remains legible and comfortable for extended reading
anti_patterns: Do not remove all color - quiet design still uses color strategically as accent (10% rule)
Do not make everything the same size and weight - hierarchy still guides user attention
Do not sacrifice usability for aesthetics - functional elements need clear affordances

faq.question: What does 'quieter' mean in design?
faq.answer: Quiet design means refined, sophisticated, and easier on the eyes. It is not boring or generic - it uses restraint to signal quality and confidence.

faq.question: Can quieter handle specific components?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify a target component or feature. The skill will focus refinement on that specific area while leaving the rest of the design unchanged.

faq.question: Does quieter work with any design system?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with CSS, Tailwind, styled-components, or any design system. It analyzes the code and provides specific recommendations for your implementation.

faq.question: How much does quieter reduce visual intensity?
faq.answer: The skill typically reduces saturation to 70-85%, adjusts typography weights down by 200-400 points, and increases spacing by 10-20% for a balanced refinement.

faq.question: Can quieter break existing functionality?
faq.answer: No, the skill only suggests design changes. It preserves all functional elements, interactive states, and usability while improving the visual experience.

faq.question: How is quieter different from just using fewer colors?
faq.answer: Quieter is comprehensive - it addresses color, typography, spacing, hierarchy, animation, and composition. It is about thoughtful restraint across all design dimensions.
